title: CC ecommerce holds above POS at 50.2% of total CC volume
body: In Mar 2026, ecommerce accounted for 50.2% (+0.1pp vs prior month) of total CC transaction volume vs POS at 49.1%. This structural shift — digital-first over in-store — has persisted for multiple months.

title: Credit cards outstanding: 5 consecutive months of growth — 118.6M cards
body: Total credit cards outstanding reached 118.6M in Mar 2026 (+2.5% QoQ vs Q3 FY26), marking the 5 consecutive months of growth. Issuance momentum is broad-based across bank types.

title: All CC transaction types surged in Mar 2026 — avg 15.8% MoM
body: All four CC transaction types grew strongly in Mar 2026: POS +16.7%, eCommerce +17.2%, ATM +11.4%, Other +18.1%. March year-end spending typically drives broad-based CC transaction growth.

title: SFB banks gained CC card share in Mar 2026 (+0.1pp)
body: SFB banks hold 1.5% of total credit cards outstanding in Mar 2026 (+0.1pp vs prior month). Private banks lost the most share at -0.1pp, now at 71.0%. SFB growth in credit cards reflects increased fintech partnerships.

title: Top 5 banks hold 74.3% of CC cards — HDFC BANK LTD leads at 22.2%
body: In Mar 2026, the top 5 banks account for 74.3% of total credit cards outstanding (-0.2pp vs prior month). HDFC BANK LTD leads with 22.2% share (+0.2% MoM).

title: DC ATM cash losing share — 81.4% of DC volume (-0.5pp) as digital grows
body: Debit card ATM withdrawals account for 81.4% of total DC transaction volume in Mar 2026 (-0.5pp vs prior month). DC ecommerce has grown to 4.8% (+0.3pp). The structural shift from cash to digital payments is underway in the debit segment.

title: DC POS cash withdrawals: 2 consecutive months of decline (-7.0% MoM)
body: Debit card POS cash-back withdrawal volume fell 7.0% MoM in Mar 2026, the 2 consecutive months of decline. This is a separate channel from ATM cash — POS cash-back usage is contracting while digital POS payments continue to grow.

title: DC ecommerce is 4.8% of total DC transaction volume in Mar 2026
body: Debit card ecommerce accounted for 4.8% of total DC transaction volume in Mar 2026 (+0.3pp vs prior month). ATM cash still dominates at 81.4%. The structural shift away from cash toward digital payments is ongoing.

title: Debit cards: 5 consecutive months of growth — 1.04B outstanding
body: Total debit cards outstanding reached 1.04B in Mar 2026 (+1.0% QoQ vs Q3 FY26), the 5 consecutive months of growth. With over 1B cards, India debit base continues to expand.

title: PSB banks hold 62.9% of debit cards — PSB gaining share in Mar 2026
body: Public sector banks account for 62.9% of total debit cards outstanding in Mar 2026 (+0.1pp vs prior month). Private banks hold 25.1%. PSB banks are the fastest-growing category (+0.1pp share gain).

title: STATE BANK OF INDIA leads DC cards at 24.2%
body: STATE BANK OF INDIA holds 24.2% of total debit cards in Mar 2026 (+0.5% MoM). Top 5 banks account for 49.8% (-0.1pp vs prior month).

title: India now has 65 UPI QR codes per POS terminal
body: As of Mar 2026, there are 65 UPI QR codes (761.2M) for every POS terminal (11.8M). This ratio was 65 in the prior month. UPI QR grew +0.9% MoM vs POS terminals at +0.9% MoM — digital acceptance infrastructure is outpacing hardware deployment.

title: POS terminals: 4 consecutive months of growth — 11.8M deployed
body: POS terminals reached 11.8M in Mar 2026 (+2.6% QoQ vs Q3 FY26), the 4 consecutive months of growth. Physical acceptance infrastructure continues to expand.

title: UPI QR codes are 135x Bharat QR in scale — 761.2M vs 5.63M
body: As of Mar 2026, there are 761.2M UPI QR codes deployed vs 5.63M Bharat QR codes — a 135x gap. UPI QR grew +0.9% MoM vs Bharat QR at -4.5% MoM. UPI has decisively won the QR standard battle in India.

title: RBL BANK LTD leads POS terminal deployment at 22.5% market share
body: RBL BANK LTD has deployed 2.65M POS terminals in Mar 2026, accounting for 22.5% of total (+9.5% MoM). Top 5 banks hold 93.1% of all POS terminals (+0.1pp vs prior month). Notable: RBL BANK LTD moved from #2 to #1.
